# Questions in Livestream 50

Live Stream #50: Terminator Edition.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:09</a> | What laws did Egyptians and Israelites have regarding grave desecration, and were they similar? | He says the two cultures handled grave desecration differently: Israelite concerns were mainly purity-related, while Egyptian concerns centered on tomb robbery and social order. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=791" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:11</a> | Was organized crime a thing in ancient Egypt and the ancient Near East? | He says organized criminal bands are clearer in the wider ancient Near East, while Egyptian crime was more often theft or loosely organized tomb robbery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=967" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:07</a> | What is your view of biblical inerrancy, and is it older than Luther? | He says the concept predates Luther, affirms inerrancy with caveats about autographs and interpretation, and generally follows the Chicago Statement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=1241" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:41</a> | In Matthew 16:28 and 26:24, can the language refer to movement or ascension rather than only the Second Coming? | He says Matthew 16:28 may point to the kingdom's inauguration and ascension, while Matthew 26:24 uses a different verb in the context of betrayal and crucifixion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=1688" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:08</a> | What actually happened in Genesis 9:20-27 with Ham and Noah? | He says "uncovering nakedness" is a euphemism for a serious sexual offense involving Noah's wife, while noting the text leaves some details unclear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=2092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:52</a> | Does an Egyptian absence record mentioning "drinking with Khonsu" refer to a festival? | He says it most likely refers to a drinking festival connected with Khonsu, though the exact festival calendar is not known. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=2319" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:39</a> | Did ancient Egyptians use sunscreen? | He says not in the modern sense; they did use cosmetics such as eye paint to reduce glare and adjusted work patterns around heat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=2607" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:27</a> | How should the `tanninim hagadolim` of Genesis 1:21 be identified? | He rejects dinosaurs, dragons, and whales, and sees the phrase as ancient Near Eastern sea-monster or deep-serpent imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=2827" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:07</a> | Why was bovine worship so widespread across the ancient world? | He points to cattle's importance for agrarian life, including milk, meat, leather, plowing, fertilizer, and cleaner farming around water supplies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=3146" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:26</a> | How should I respond when young-earth creationists call someone a heretic for doubting a 6,000-year-old earth? | He says the age of the earth is not an essential of the Christian faith and should not be treated as heresy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=3539" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:59</a> | If Jesus and Joshua have the same Greek name, why are both not called Jesus in English? | He says English preserves two translational traditions: Joshua from Hebrew and Jesus through Greek and Latin, partly to distinguish Jesus Christ from other Joshuas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=3769" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:49</a> | Did Moses think he could gain power over God by knowing God's secret name at the burning bush? | He says that idea fits Egyptian magic generally, but the Moses narrative presents Moses as God's servant rather than someone manipulating God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=3965" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:05</a> | Is Genesis 1:1 better translated "In the beginning God created" or "When God began to create"? | He prefers "In the beginning God created," saying the subordinate-clause translation rests on shaky linguistic grounds even if verse 1 can function as a title. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=4502" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:02</a> | Is the Tale of Sinuhe connected with the story of Joseph? | He says the similarities are probably coincidence; Sinuhe moves from Egypt to the Levant, Joseph's setting is later, and Sinuhe may reflect a real tomb-biography tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=4945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:25</a> | What are your views on the persons of Adam and Eve? | He says he regards Adam and Eve as historical people and archetypes, with Adam also functioning as a clan progenitor in the narrative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=5106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:06</a> | What kind of governments in Canaan did Israel go against? | He says Canaan was made up largely of small city-states ruled by kings or chiefs, not one unified empire or nation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=5389" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:49</a> | Is Mark 14:62 also about the ascension? | He says yes, treating it as another ascension-related text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=5447" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:47</a> | Were ladders used in some Egyptian art for Osiris, and is there a Jacob's ladder connection? | He says Egyptians certainly had and used ladders, including for tomb and temple work, but he is unsure about the specific Osiris-art claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=5756" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:56</a> | Does the Greek myth of Io relate to Hathor? | He says it is possible because some Greek myth has Egyptian connections, but he does not know Greek myth well enough to answer confidently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=5895" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:15</a> | What do you think of the claim that Daniel is a 165 BCE forgery? | He rejects that dating, saying the argument often assumes prophecy cannot happen and that objections to Daniel's historicity have possible explanations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=6255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:15</a> | Is Kathleen Kenyon's dating of Jericho's destruction layer accurate, and was it disturbed by erosion or site leveling? | He says Kenyon's dating of City IV's middle bronze destruction layer is solid, while later bronze layers were more affected by erosion or leveling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=6611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:11</a> | Is Jacob's ladder Egyptian? | He says it could be, but it does not need to be, since Canaanites and Israelites also knew and used ladders. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=6716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:56</a> | Was Seth the biological son of Adam or a later progenitor from Adam's clan? | He says Seth could be a progenitor in Adam's line, because ancient Israelite father-son language could span generations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=6926" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:26</a> | Did you have a good May 4th, and is there any new Egyptology news? | He says no specific new Egyptology news comes to mind, then notes the channel has reached its second anniversary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=7073" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:53</a> | Would nuclear war mean the end times would have to start during such a war? | He says not necessarily; nuclear weapons are devastating, especially to cities, but should not automatically be read as starting an end-times scenario. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=7375" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:55</a> | Where should a layman start to learn more about Akhenaten and Atenism? | He recommends Aidan Dodson's `Amarna Sunset` and mentions a forthcoming article, while disagreeing with the view that Atenism was monotheism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/VeCTayU7MpQ?t=7694" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:14</a> | Can we identify which Egyptian deity each plague is polemicizing against? | He says no; he thinks the plague-deity matching view overreads Exodus 12:12 and that the plagues are judgment on Pharaoh rather than a deity-by-deity polemic. |
